description:
Goal: make the production Compiler2 JIT/AOT entry points consume `NativeProgram(root)` end-to-end. Signal: Compiler2-native JIT/AOT runs execute through the shared native path without `prepare_preplanned_native`, `materialize_program`, `AbiFacts::derive`, `plan_module_with_role`, or `resolve_module_types`, and the temporary test-only native consumer seam is gone.

Change:
- add production Compiler2 JIT/AOT front doors that demand `LowerNativeProgram(root)` and hand `NativeProgram(root)` to shared native codegen
- fold the current test-only `compile_with_backend_native_program` / `prepare_native_codegen_surface_from_compiler2` path into the real native consumer entry, or delete it if the public path makes it redundant
- remove the remaining Compiler2-side dependence on old native-preparation helpers while wiring the outer consumer switch

Why:
- the native codegen seam is already carved and the lowerers already consume Compiler2-native facts; the remaining work should now be only the public consumer move and cleanup

Checks:
- Compiler2 JIT and AOT contract tests pass for quicksort, `Enum.reduce`, and variadic externs
- telemetry/assertions prove the forbidden old-world helpers were not used
- no temporary Compiler2-native test-only consumer path remains once the production entry points exist
title: Compiler2 native backends: move shared codegen and JIT/AOT entry paths onto the new handoff
